prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui indices f⁺/f⁻/dual for nitrobenzene; headline value is the largest electrophilic index f⁺.
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`) in PySCF ‒ exact four-center integrals (density-fitting OFF).
• Geometry: single-point calculations on the supplied fixed geometry /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/nitrobenzene.xyz (no further optimisation); N, N+1 and N−1 states solved separately.
• Solvent/environment: gas phase (no implicit continuum).
• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (0, 1); cation and anion treated as doublets (±1, 2).
• Software: chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill → PySCF backend.
• Convergence & caveats: all SCFs converged; integrity status ok, trustworthy true.  Automated charge-conservation checks returned f⁺ and f⁻ sums of exactly 1.0000 (see warnings below).

Headline result
Most electrophilic atom: C-4 (index 3) with f⁺ = 0.49641779.
Most nucleophilic atom: C-5 (index 4) with f⁻ = 0.10728820.
Dual descriptor also flags C-4 as the dominant electrophilic site (+0.51070983).
